summary: Russian forces launched drone attacks on Ukrainian fuel infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, targeting Ukrnafta gas stations and Naftogaz production facilities. Ukrainian authorities reported that Russian forces are concentrating significant air defense assets around Moscow and the Kerch Strait area at the expense of other regions. The SBU reported strikes on Russian military airfields in Sak and Hvardiyske in temporarily occupied Crimea, as well as S-400 and Pantsir-S1 systems near Kerch.
key_points: Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, and Naftogaz production facilities in Poltava region, causing station shutdowns (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).

Zelenskyy stated Russia is pulling air defense systems toward Moscow and the Kerch Strait from other directions, concentrating hundreds of S-400, S-500, and Pantsir launchers in the Moscow region and nearly 90 launchers in Valdai (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).

SBU reported that Alpha Special Operations Center struck Russian military airfields at Sak and Hvardiyske in Crimea, hitting four aircraft storage hangars, two S-400 units, and two Pantsir-S1 systems near Kerch (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).

Russian air defense claimed to have shot down 245 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea water area in one day, according to the Russian Defense Ministry (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Russian 'Geranium' drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for the Armed Forces of Ukraine in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Hungary blocked the EU's common position needed to open new negotiating clusters on Ukraine and Moldova's EU accession, becoming the only country to do so (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31116).

NATO Secretary General Rutte called for continued pressure on Putin to negotiate, while Zelenskyy stated Russian intelligence is proposing to postpone or cancel the September 'United Russia' State Duma election ritual (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31137, @UkraineNow/82158).

The US Department of Defense will create two training grounds to simulate conflict conditions in Ukraine for electronic warfare training and joint work between drone manufacturers and countermeasure developers (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31121).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched coordinated drone strikes against Ukrainian energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Poltava regions, targeting Ukrnafta and Naftogaz facilities. Ukrainian intelligence reported that Russia is stripping air defense assets from other regions to concentrate massive S-400, S-500, and Pantsir deployments around Moscow and the Kerch Strait. The SBU claimed successful strikes on Russian military airfields in occupied Crimea, hitting air defense systems and aircraft hangars near Sak, Hvardiyske, and Kerch.

points: Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, as well as Naftogaz production assets in Poltava region, causing gas station operations to halt (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).

SBU reported that Alpha Special Operations Center struck Russian military airfields at Sak and Hvardiyske in temporarily occupied Crimea, with preliminary reports indicating four aircraft storage hangars were hit at Sak airfield (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Near Kerch, two S-400 weapons units and two Pantsir-S1 anti-aircraft missile-gun complexes were reportedly struck in the Kerch Strait area (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Zelenskyy stated that retranslators on Belarusian territory are currently not operating, confirming this as a fact (Source: @UkraineNow/82159).

A 24-year-old Norwegian People's Aid demining specialist was killed and four other workers were injured, two in critical condition, as a result of Russian shelling of Novopetrivka in Vysokopillya community, Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/82149).

Zelenskyy stated that Ukraine's operation regarding Crimea is calculated and that if specific capabilities discussed with G7 partners are provided, Ukraine will quickly create conditions forcing Russia to choose peace (Source: @UkraineNow/82155).

Zelenskyy stated that Russian intelligence services are proposing to postpone or cancel the September 'United Russia' State Duma election, citing internal uncertainty in Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/82158).
Russian Defense Ministry claimed air defense means shot down 245 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea water area in one day (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Russian 'Geranium' drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for the Armed Forces of Ukraine in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).

Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak reported to Putin that oil refineries are operating at maximum capacity and previously unused reserve mechanisms had to be activated to stabilize domestic gasoline supply, according to The Moscow Times (Source: @UkraineNow/82141).

Ulyanovsk Oblast will impose fuel sales restrictions starting June 25: 40 liters of gasoline per vehicle, 100 liters of diesel per passenger car, and 300 liters for trucks and buses (Source: @tass_agency/382265).

Putin stated that Russia has fully import-substituted all aviation technology and inspected new Il-114-300, SSJ-100, and MS-21 aircraft at the Gromov Flight Research Institute (Source: @tass_agency/382260, @tass_agency/382231).
Lavrov stated that the introduction of stabilization forces to Ukraine, as sought by Europeans, would result in a new Berlin Wall (Source: @tass_agency/382249).
Medvedev expressed confidence in achieving the goals of the 'special military operation' on the battlefield at the St. Petersburg International Legal Forum (Source: @tass_agency/382253).
Russian 'Geranium' drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian forces in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
A reconnaissance UAV was reported in the northern part of Sloviansk, with means deployed to shoot it down (Source: @kpszsu/66216).
UAVs were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the north, and from Belgorod Oblast of Russia toward the south of Sumy region, course set for Krasnopillya (Source: @kpszsu/66199).
In Kharkiv Oblast, a rocket strike hit Musiyky and a strike UAV hit Ruska Lozova (Source: @eRadarrua/98018).
UAVs were reported near/past Savintsi in Kharkiv Oblast heading toward Balakliya (Source: @kpszsu/66214).
UAVs were reported heading toward Pechenihy in Kharkiv Oblast from the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/66226).
A rocket-type UAV was reported from Kursk Oblast of Russia heading toward Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/66243).
UAVs were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66199).
UAVs from Belgorod Oblast of Russia were reported heading toward the south of Sumy region, course set for Krasnopillya (Source: @kpszsu/66199).
Lightning-type UAVs were reported heading toward Sumy from the north, course set for Romny in Sumy Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98066).
A rocket-type UAV was reported from Kursk Oblast of Russia heading toward Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/66243).
UAVs were reported heading toward Pechenihy in Kharkiv Oblast from the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/66226).
UAVs were reported near/past Savintsi in Kharkiv Oblast heading toward Balakliya (Source: @kpszsu/66214).
SBU reported strikes on Russian military airfields at Sak and Hvardiyske in temporarily occupied Crimea, with four aircraft storage hangars reportedly hit at Sak (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Near Kerch, two S-400 weapons units and two Pantsir-S1 anti-aircraft missile-gun complexes were struck (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).

A 24-year-old Norwegian People's Aid demining specialist was killed and four workers injured from Russian shelling of Novopetrivka in Vysokopillya community, Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/82149).
A 'blackout' occurred in the TOT of Kherson Oblast, with Gauleiter Saldo stating all districts were partially or fully de-energized (Source: @UkraineNow/82143).
UAVs were reported on the western outskirts of Kherson heading northeast (Source: @kpszsu/66232).
UAVs were reported heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/66231).
Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia and Mykolaiv oblasts, causing station shutdowns (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Russian Defense Ministry claimed air defense shot down 245 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, and Naftogaz facilities in Poltava region (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Russian 'Geranium' drones hit railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian forces in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).

SBU Alpha Special Operations Center struck Russian military airfields at Sak and Hvardiyske in Crimea, hitting four aircraft hangars, two S-400 units, and two Pantsir-S1 systems near Kerch (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).

Zelenskyy stated Russia concentrated hundreds of S-400, S-500, and Pantsir launchers in the Moscow region and nearly 90 launchers in Valdai, stripping air defense from other directions (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).
In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, one Russian UAV and three strike UAV groups were reported; in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, one strike UAV group was reported (Source: @eRadarrua/98023).
A reconnaissance UAV was reported near Aviatorske in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98053).

key_developments: Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, and Naftogaz production facilities in Poltava region, causing station shutdowns (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).

SBU Alpha Special Operations Center struck Russian military airfields at Sak and Hvardiyske in Crimea, hitting four aircraft hangars, two S-400 units, and two Pantsir-S1 systems near Kerch (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).

Zelenskyy stated Russia is stripping air defense from other regions to concentrate hundreds of S-400, S-500, and Pantsir launchers around Moscow and nearly 90 launchers in Valdai (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).
Russian Defense Ministry claimed air defense shot down 245 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Russian 'Geranium' drones struck railway trains transporting military supplies for Ukrainian forces in the Kharkov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Hungary blocked the EU's common position needed to open new negotiating clusters on Ukraine's EU accession (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31116).

The US Department of Defense will create two training grounds to simulate conflict conditions in Ukraine for electronic warfare and joint drone-countermeasure development (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31121).
sections.threat_assessment: The Russian military is conducting sustained strikes against Ukrainian energy infrastructure, targeting Ukrnafta and Naftogaz facilities across multiple oblasts including Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Poltava. Russia is simultaneously concentrating significant air defense assets around Moscow and the Kerch Strait at the expense of other regions, suggesting a prioritization of leadership protection over force protection elsewhere. Ukrainian special operations continue to strike high-value targets in occupied Crimea, including S-400 and Pantsir-S1 systems, while Russian long-range drone strikes against logistics infrastructure in Kharkov region indicate continued efforts to disrupt Ukrainian military supply lines.
sections.regional_response: Hungary blocked the EU's common position required to open new negotiating clusters on Ukraine's EU accession, becoming the only member state to do so. NATO Secretary General Rutte called for continued pressure on Putin to negotiate, noting Ukraine is showing good results. Slovak President Fico stated he would ensure Slovakia does not participate in military loans to Ukraine, responding to a NATO proposal for approximately €70 billion in annual military aid to Ukraine. Trump is reportedly inclined to increase support for Kyiv and exert more pressure on Russia regarding ending the conflict, according to the Financial Times.
sections.intelligence_notes: Russian intelligence services are reportedly proposing to postpone or cancel the September 'United Russia' State Duma election due to internal uncertainty, according to Zelenskyy. The Kremlin has acknowledged serious problems with domestic gasoline supply, with refineries operating at maximum capacity and previously unused reserve mechanisms activated. Russian Deputy Prime Minister Novak reported these findings to Putin, according to The Moscow Times. A Norwegian People's Aid demining specialist was killed and four workers injured from Russian shelling in Kherson Oblast. The US Department of Defense plans to create two training grounds simulating Ukrainian conflict conditions for electronic warfare training and joint drone-countermeasure development.
